mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has been recovering since April 2020 lows
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals improving economic conditions and increased consumer confidence, which typically translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has fallen from its April 2020 peak of 14.7% to 5.7% by July 2020
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE drops by >0.5pp over 3 months during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains low despite extremely low federal funds rates, it suggests that monetary policy is accommodative without immediately triggering fears of overheating, providing a supportive environment for equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.11% and CPIAUCSL is 236.149 (low inflation)
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is below 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, a rebound in housing starts indicates renewed consumer and business confidence, stimulating economic activity and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as sentiment improves rapidly.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST has rebounded to 1089k in June 2020 from April 2020 lows
failure_condition: If HOUST rising by >10% MoM during post-shock recoveries does not correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above a low threshold signals a shift towards monetary tightening, which typically increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% and stays above for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is currently 0.11%, indicating extreme easing
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer demand and economic health, directly supporting corporate revenues and earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon due to immediate market reaction.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS has shown strong recovery in recent months, reaching 439771M in July 2020
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS growth and 5-day SPX returns is consistently negative (Rank IC < -0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and declining unemployment signifies a robust and expanding economy, boosting investor confidence and corporate outlooks, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized AND UNRATE falls by > 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was negative in Q1 2020, UNRATE is recovering from COVID peak
failure_condition: If periods meeting the GDPC1 growth and UNRATE decline conditions do not show significantly positive 60-day SPX returns (t-stat < 1.5) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery following a downturn, a sustained rebound in industrial production indicates that supply chains are normalizing and manufacturing activity is picking up, which is a strong positive signal for corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been recovering since April 2020 lows, reaching 106.5114 in June 2020
failure_condition: If INDPRO increasing for 3 consecutive months during post-recession periods does not lead to positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 65% of instances.
